possible_choices: The normality assumption of the t-test may be violated.
A paired test would be more suitable because pre- and post-test data are paired.
A correlation analysis would be better to analyze the relation of pre- and post-test results.
The independence assumption of the t-test may be violated.
choice_explanations: This may be a problem. While the t-test does not assume that the data is normally distributed, it requires that the means of the samples are - which may be false.
This may be a problem. With an unpaired test, the variance between students may occlude our results.
This would have been the correct answer. We are looking for a significant difference between pre- and post-test results. A correlation analysis would not help for this question.
This may always be a problem, although this is a problem for almost all statistical tests, not only the t-test.
